metadata.title: Reflection paper on regulatory requirements for the development of medicinal products for primary biliary cholangitis (PBC) and primary sclerosing cholangitis (PSC)

rules.rule_text: This document should be read in conjunction with the introduction and general principles and part I and II of the Annex I to Directive 2001/83/EC as amended.
rules.verbatim_quote: This document should be read in conjunction with the introduction and general principles and part I and II of the Annex I to Directive 2001/83/EC as amended.

rules.rule_text: Applicants should also refer to other relevant EU and ICH guidelines and regulations.
rules.verbatim_quote: Applicants should also refer to other relevant EU and ICH guidelines and regulations, especially the following:

rules.rule_text: Scientific advice is recommended in case a particular topic about PBC and/or PSC is not covered in this reflection paper.
rules.verbatim_quote: Scientific advice is recommended in case a particular topic about PBC and/or PSC is not covered in this reflection paper.

rules.rule_text: For patients already being treated with UDCA, patients should be randomised to either continue treatment with UDCA or to the new treatment.
rules.source_section: 4.2.1. Study design and comparators — 4.2.1.1. First-line therapy as alternative to UDCA
rules.verbatim_quote: For patients already being treated with UDCA, patients should be randomised to either continue treatment with UDCA or to the new treatment.

rules.rule_text: If the study population includes patients with a satisfactory response to UDCA (complete responders), the protocol has to ensure appropriate monitoring and rescue therapy in the case of a loss of response.
rules.source_section: 4.2.1. Study design and comparators — 4.2.1.1. First-line therapy as alternative to UDCA
rules.verbatim_quote: If the study population includes patients with a satisfactory response to UDCA (complete responders), the protocol has to ensure appropriate monitoring and rescue therapy in the case of a loss of response.

rules.rule_text: Studies in the add-on setting are recommended to be conducted with placebo-control only.
rules.source_section: 4.2.1. Study design and comparators — 4.2.1.2. Second-line therapy (monotherapy or add-on to UDCA)
rules.verbatim_quote: Studies in the add-on setting are recommended to be conducted with placebo-control only.

rules.rule_text: It is recommended to study patients with different stages of the disease, i.e. both early PBC and advanced PBC.
rules.verbatim_quote: It is recommended to study patients with different stages of the disease, i.e. both early PBC and advanced PBC.

rules.rule_text: Patients with AIH-PBC overlap syndrome should normally be excluded from studies by use of relevant criteria for the diagnosis of this sub-group.
rules.verbatim_quote: Patients with AIH-PBC overlap syndrome should normally be excluded from studies by use of relevant criteria for the diagnosis of this sub-group.
exceptions: Patients intolerant or unresponsive to second-line treatments can be included, in which case these treatment(s) would then be replaced by the investigative agent

rules.rule_text: The relevant patient population should be stated in the 'Population' attribute of the estimand(s) targeted by the study.
rules.verbatim_quote: The relevant patient population should be stated in the 'Population' attribute of the estimand(s) targeted by the study.

rules.rule_text: Treatment naive patients should be included based on an unequivocal diagnosis of PBC associated with a defined minimal increase in the biochemical markers of the disease, especially ALP with or without bilirubin elevation, allowing for relevant improvements.
rules.source_section: 4.2.2. Selection of patient populations — 4.2.2.1. First-line therapy as alternative to UDCA
rules.verbatim_quote: Treatment naive patients should be included based on an unequivocal diagnosis of PBC associated with a defined minimal increase in the biochemical markers of the disease, especially ALP with or without bilirubin elevation, allowing for relevant improvements.

rules.rule_text: The choice of criteria for complete or partial response at baseline will have to be justified based on literature.
rules.source_section: 4.2.2. Selection of patient populations — 4.2.2.1. First-line therapy as alternative to UDCA
rules.verbatim_quote: The choice of criteria for complete or partial response at baseline will have to be justified based on literature.

rules.rule_text: The availability of a baseline histology evaluation as well as follow-up evaluation in at least a sub-group of patients is recommended.
rules.source_section: 4.2.2. Selection of patient populations — 4.2.2.1. First-line therapy as alternative to UDCA
rules.verbatim_quote: The availability of a baseline histology evaluation as well as follow-up evaluation in at least a sub-group of patients is recommended.

rules.rule_text: It is recommended that more strict criteria are chosen, allowing only those patients into the study which have still a relevant alteration of the serological markers of PBC.
rules.source_section: 4.2.2. Selection of patient populations — 4.2.2.2. Second-line therapy in patients unresponsive or intolerant to UDCA
rules.verbatim_quote: it is recommended that more strict criteria are chosen, allowing only those patients into the study which have still a relevant alteration of the serological markers of PBC.
exceptions: Selection of patients based on the combined use of ALP≥1.67xULN, and bilirubin within the normal range of < 2xULN after at least a 1-year therapy with UDCA at the standard recommended dose (12-16 mg/kg/day) is acceptable

rules.rule_text: Non-invasive tests, e.g. for evaluating liver fibrosis stage are recommended to be used for further enrichment and/or stratification.
rules.source_section: 4.2.2. Selection of patient populations — 4.2.2.2. Second-line therapy in patients unresponsive or intolerant to UDCA
rules.verbatim_quote: Non-invasive tests, e.g. for evaluating liver fibrosis stage are recommended to be used for further enrichment and/or stratification.

rules.rule_text: Patients with different stages of the disease should be studied, either in separate studies or in the same study.
rules.source_section: 4.2.2. Selection of patient populations — 4.2.2.2. Second-line therapy in patients unresponsive or intolerant to UDCA
rules.verbatim_quote: Patients with different stages of the disease should be studied, either in separate studies or in the same study.

rules.rule_text: The availability of a baseline histology evaluation as well as follow-up evaluation in at least a sub-group of patients, is highly recommended in patients non-responsive to UDCA.
rules.source_section: 4.2.2. Selection of patient populations — 4.2.2.2. Second-line therapy in patients unresponsive or intolerant to UDCA
rules.verbatim_quote: The availability of a baseline histology evaluation as well as follow-up evaluation in at least a sub-group of patients, is highly recommended in patients non-responsive to UDCA.

rules.rule_text: In the first-line setting, a relevant endpoint would be the composite endpoint of the normalisation of the relevant biochemical markers, mainly ALP and total bilirubin (as composite endpoint at the individual level). Any deviation from this stringent definition should be justified.
rules.source_section: 4.2.3. Endpoints and summary measure — 4.2.3.1. First-line therapy as alternative to UDCA
rules.verbatim_quote: In the first-line setting, a relevant endpoint would be the composite endpoint of the normalisation of the relevant biochemical markers, mainly ALP and total bilirubin (as composite endpoint at the individual level). Any deviation from this stringent definition should be justified.

rules.rule_text: A responder analysis is recommended, i.e. percentage of responders in study drug vs control group based on a pre-defined definition of a responder.
rules.source_section: 4.2.3. Endpoints and summary measure — 4.2.3.1. First-line therapy as alternative to UDCA (Summary measure)
rules.verbatim_quote: A responder analysis is recommended, i.e. percentage of responders in study drug vs control group based on a pre-defined definition of a responder.

rules.rule_text: For non-inferiority studies, a margin needs to be defined and justified based on natural history data and clinical study experience with UDCA.
rules.source_section: 4.2.3. Endpoints and summary measure — 4.2.3.1. First-line therapy as alternative to UDCA (Summary measure)
rules.verbatim_quote: For non-inferiority studies, a margin needs to be defined and justified based on natural history data and clinical study experience with UDCA.

rules.rule_text: The clinical relevance of each of the supportive endpoints should be substantiated.
rules.source_section: 4.2.3. Endpoints and summary measure — 4.2.3.1. First-line therapy (Supportive endpoints)
rules.verbatim_quote: The clinical relevance of each of these endpoints should be substantiated.

rules.rule_text: The study duration would need to be at least 2 years, with extended (controlled) follow-up to be planned.
rules.source_section: 4.2.3. Endpoints and summary measure — 4.2.3.1. First-line therapy (Study duration)
rules.verbatim_quote: The study duration would need to be at least 2 years, with extended (controlled) follow-up to be planned.

rules.rule_text: An endpoint of complete response, i.e. normalisation of ALP and a bilirubin level of < 0.7xULN is highly recommended as a primary or secondary endpoint in second-line therapy.
rules.source_section: 4.2.3. Endpoints and summary measure — 4.2.3.2. Second-line therapy (monotherapy or add-on to UDCA)
rules.verbatim_quote: An endpoint of complete response, i.e. normalisation of ALP and a bilirubin level of < 0.7xULN is highly recommended as a primary or secondary endpoint.

rules.rule_text: Long-term outcome data with respect to the diagnosis of cirrhosis, decompensation events of cirrhosis, MELD score of > 14 defining a high risk of liver related death, as well as liver transplantation and death should form the basis for a long-term follow-up evaluation of efficacy.
rules.source_section: 4.2.3. Endpoints and summary measure — 4.2.3.2. Second-line therapy (Supportive endpoints)
rules.verbatim_quote: it would usually be expected that long-term outcome data with respect to the diagnosis of cirrhosis (based on histology or liver stiffness measurement), decompensation events of cirrhosis, MELD score of > 14 defining a high risk of liver related death, as well as liver transplantation and death should form the basis for a long-term follow-up evaluation of efficacy.
exceptions: Not feasible where fully powered studies using clinical outcomes are not possible

rules.rule_text: The applicants will also have to take care that the best possible evidence with regard to secondary evaluations is also available, including but not limited to non-invasive measurements of liver fibrosis/stiffness, biochemical markers of inflammation and liver function, as well as histology.
rules.source_section: 4.2.3. Endpoints and summary measure — 4.2.3.2. Second-line therapy (Supportive endpoints)
rules.verbatim_quote: This should include, but is not restricted to, non-invasive measurements of liver fibrosis/stiffness, biochemical markers of inflammation and liver function, as well as histology.

rules.rule_text: It would be expected that all intercurrent events are pre-defined in the study protocol and compared between the treatment arms.
rules.verbatim_quote: It would be expected that all intercurrent events are pre-defined in the study protocol and compared between the treatment arms.

rules.rule_text: The intake of rescue medication, or liver-related events and death from any cause should be considered as a treatment failure, i.e. the composite strategy as discussed in the addendum is considered appropriate for these intercurrent events.
rules.verbatim_quote: The intake of rescue medication, or liver-related events and death from any cause should be considered as a treatment failure, i.e. the composite strategy as discussed in the addendum is considered appropriate for these intercurrent events.

rules.rule_text: For other intercurrent events, including those related to lack of adherence to treatment, the 'treatment-policy' strategy should be applied, i.e. the outcome regardless of the intercurrent event is of primary interest.
rules.verbatim_quote: in general, unless an alternative is justified, for other intercurrent events, including those related to lack of adherence to treatment, the "treatment-policy" strategy should be applied, i.e. the outcome regardless of the intercurrent event is of primary interest.

rules.rule_text: Choices made for study design, data collection and statistical analysis should be aligned to the scientific question of interest that is posed by the study objective, requiring a detailed specification of the estimand (the 'target of estimation'), including the specification of strategies to handle each of the relevant events that occur after randomisation and that would affect the interpretation of an outcome variable or preclude its observation (intercurrent events).
rules.verbatim_quote: Choices made for study design, data collection and statistical analysis should be aligned to the scientific question of interest that is posed by the study objective. This requires a detailed specification of the estimand (the "target of estimation"), including the specification of strategies to handle each of the relevant events that occur after randomisation and that would affect the interpretation of an outcome variable or preclude its observation (intercurrent events).

rules.rule_text: Sensitivity analyses to support the robustness of the primary analysis should be provided.
rules.verbatim_quote: Generally, sensitivity analyses to support the robustness of the primary analysis should be provided.

rules.rule_text: Use of real-world evidence, e.g. registries to provide pivotal evidence for an application is generally not recommended but would be acceptable as an explorative approach.
rules.verbatim_quote: Use of real-world evidence, e.g. registries to provide pivotal evidence for an application is generally not recommended but would be acceptable as an explorative approach.

rules.rule_text: Consultation with the paediatric committee PDCO is required for development of new substances in the treatment of PBC in children and adolescents.
rules.verbatim_quote: Nevertheless, consultation with the paediatric committee PDCO is required.

rules.rule_text: Special attention should be paid to liver safety considering the target organ and the additional risk of liver toxicity in patients with underlying liver disease.
rules.verbatim_quote: Apart from general safety monitoring, special attention should be paid to liver safety considering the target organ and the additional risk of liver toxicity in patients with underlying liver disease.

rules.rule_text: It is recommended to include in the clinical study protocol clear rules for the management and assessment of significant liver adverse events, including potential Hy's Law cases.
rules.verbatim_quote: It is recommended to include in the clinical study protocol clear rules for the management and assessment of significant liver adverse events, including potential Hy´s Law cases.

rules.rule_text: A safety data monitoring committee, including one or more hepatology experts is recommended.
rules.verbatim_quote: A safety data monitoring committee, including one or more hepatology experts is recommended.

rules.rule_text: Causality assessment of serious liver adverse events, including potential Hy's Law cases should use expert adjudication.
rules.verbatim_quote: Causality assessment of serious liver adverse events, including potential Hy´s Law cases should use expert adjudication.

rules.rule_text: Exclusion criteria related to liver biochemistry, monitoring of liver tests during the study and stopping criteria should be defined in a way to timely identify and reduce any risk related to liver toxicity.
rules.verbatim_quote: exclusion criteria e.g. related to liver biochemistry, monitoring of liver tests during the study and stopping criteria should be defined in a way to timely identify and reduce any risk related to liver toxicity.

rules.rule_text: According to ICH E9(R1) addendum on estimands and sensitivity analysis in clinical studies, the scientific question of interest should be specified, and study features should be aligned.
rules.source_section: 5.2. Patient population, study design and endpoints
rules.verbatim_quote: According to ICH E9(R1) addendum on estimands and sensitivity analysis in clinical studies (EMA/CHMP/ICH/436221/2017), the scientific question of interest should be specified, and study features should be aligned.

rules.rule_text: The diagnosis of PSC should be established based on the combination of elevated ALP and an abnormal cholangiography consistent with PSC, preferably by high-quality MRI/MRCP.
rules.verbatim_quote: The diagnosis of PSC should be established based on the combination of elevated ALP and an abnormal cholangiography consistent with PSC, preferably by high-quality MRI/MRCP.

rules.rule_text: Secondary reasons for sclerosing cholangitis need to be excluded, including IgG4 related sclerosing cholangitis. Patients with AIH overlap syndrome should be excluded from confirmatory studies in PSC.
rules.verbatim_quote: Secondary reasons for sclerosing cholangitis need to be excluded, including IgG4 related sclerosing cholangitis. Patients with AIH overlap syndrome should be excluded from confirmatory studies in PSC.

rules.rule_text: For inclusion into studies, minimal threshold for ALP and bilirubin elevations should be defined and justified. The applicant is advised to seek scientific advice on the inclusion criteria.
rules.verbatim_quote: For inclusion into studies, minimal threshold for ALP and bilirubin elevations should be defined and justified. Thresholds for inclusion into studies in PSC are not established. Therefore, the applicant is advised to seek scientific advice on the inclusion criteria.

rules.rule_text: In general, patients should not have relevant recent fluctuations of serum markers, and not have relevant cholestasis at inclusion.
rules.verbatim_quote: In general, patients should not have relevant recent fluctuations of serum markers, and not have relevant cholestasis at inclusion.

rules.rule_text: A relevant time-period of at least 6 months from the last flare should be defined as eligibility criterion. Concurrent antibiotic therapy should not be part of the medication at inclusion.
rules.verbatim_quote: a relevant time-period of at least 6 months from the last flare should be defined. Concurrent antibiotic therapy should not be part of the medication at inclusion.
exceptions: Previous flares of cholangitis should not be an exclusion criterion

rules.rule_text: Patients with strictures needing endoscopic/surgical intervention should be excluded.
rules.verbatim_quote: Patients with strictures needing endoscopic/surgical intervention should be excluded.

rules.rule_text: The presence of CCA or gallbladder carcinoma, as well as colorectal carcinoma should be excluded to the extent possible.
rules.verbatim_quote: The presence of CCA or gallbladder carcinoma, as well as colorectal carcinoma should be excluded to the extent possible.

rules.rule_text: It is recommended to stratify randomisation based on IBD (presence or absence). Usually, patients with active IBD should not be included as it may confound any assessment with regard to biochemical markers and symptoms.
rules.verbatim_quote: it is recommended to stratify randomisation based on IBD (presence or absence). Usually, patients with active IBD should not be included as it may confound any assessment with regard to biochemical markers and symptoms.
exceptions: Patients with concomitant IBD may be included

rules.rule_text: Concomitant medication for IBD would require to be stable for a relevant time-frame and should remain stable throughout the study duration.
rules.verbatim_quote: Similarly, concomitant medication for IBD would require to be stable for a relevant time-frame and should remain stable throughout the study duration.

rules.rule_text: The inclusion of patients on concomitant UDCA can be allowed, but intake of UDCA should not be altered during the study and the randomisation should be stratified with respect to UDCA treatment at baseline.
rules.verbatim_quote: the inclusion of patients on concomitant UDCA can be allowed, but intake of UDCA should not be altered during the study and the randomisation should be stratified with respect to UDCA treatment at baseline.

rules.rule_text: A combined use of histology evaluation and ALP changes as co-primary endpoints are regarded to represent an acceptable intermediate endpoint for PSC for the time being, reflecting both aspects of the disease.
rules.verbatim_quote: Therefore, a combined use of histology evaluation and ALP changes as co-primary endpoints are regarded to represent an acceptable intermediate endpoint for the disease for the time being, reflecting both aspects of the disease, i.e. bile duct pathology and fibrosis.
exceptions: Use of other methods for assessing fibrosis e.g. transient elastography or high-quality MRI will have to be justified

rules.rule_text: A biochemical response should be defined as a reduction of ALP to < 1.5xULN, or a combination of the reduction to < 1.5xULN with an at least 40% reduction from baseline. The histological response should be defined based on at least 1 point improvement in the fibrosis stage.
rules.verbatim_quote: it is suggested that a responder-type evaluation based on the criteria of therapeutic response should be the basis, defining biochemical response as a reduction of ALP to < 1.5xULN, or a combination of the reduction to < 1.5xULN with an at least 40% reduction from baseline. For the histological evaluation, based on one of the accepted staging system, a similar responder-type evaluation is proposed. The response should be defined based on an at least 1 point improvement in the fibrosis stage.
exceptions: Stable disease (no worsening of fibrosis) could be used instead, if adequately justified

rules.rule_text: The co-primary endpoints should be supplemented with relevant secondary endpoints, including an endpoint measuring symptom improvement (e.g. pruritus, fatigue).
rules.verbatim_quote: The co-primary endpoints should be supplemented with relevant secondary endpoints, including an endpoint measuring symptom improvement (e.g. pruritus, fatigue).

rules.rule_text: A later evaluation of long-term outcomes is considered necessary for PSC, which should be done as a composite endpoint including the manifestation of cirrhosis, a MELD score above 14, decompensation events (such as encephalopathy, variceal bleeding, and ascites), as well as liver transplantation and death.
rules.verbatim_quote: A later evaluation of long-term outcomes is also considered necessary for PSC, which should be done as a composite endpoint including the manifestation of cirrhosis, a MELD score above 14, decompensation events (such as encephalopathy, variceal bleeding, and ascites), as well as liver transplantation and death.

rules.rule_text: Future applicants should therefore also take care that a sufficient amount of supportive evidence for long-term efficacy is available, consisting of standard evaluations such as imaging modalities (MRI/MRCP, transient elastography), other biomarkers (bilirubin, transaminases, but also other potential future biomarkers) as well as important clinical events.
rules.verbatim_quote: Future applicants should therefore also take care that a sufficient amount of supportive evidence for long-term efficacy is available. This should consist of standard evaluations such as imaging modalities (MRI/MRCP, transient elastography), other biomarkers (bilirubin, transaminases, but also other potential future biomarkers) as well as important clinical events in the course of the disease, such as the number of bouts of acute cholangitis, occurrence of dominant/relevant stricture, and finally the occurrence of CCA, and other malignancies.

rules.rule_text: Choices made regarding design and statistical analysis, including the handling of missing data, must be made considering the target of estimation. The same recommendations as outlined in section 4.2.5 above apply.
rules.verbatim_quote: Choices made regarding design and statistical analysis, including the handling of missing data, must be made considering the target of estimation. The same recommendations as outlined in section 4.2.5 above apply.

rules.rule_text: Consultation with the agency early in the drug development (scientific advice and submission of a paediatric investigational plan (PIP)) is therefore advisable for PSC in children and adolescents.
rules.verbatim_quote: Consultation with the agency early in the drug development (scientific advice and submission of a paediatric investigational plan (PIP)) is therefore advisable.

rules.rule_text: Prior to inclusion, every effort should be made to exclude malignancy by recent imaging, endoscopy and laboratory markers, including tumour markers such as CA 19-9. Monitoring should be in place for these malignancies.
rules.verbatim_quote: Prior to inclusion, every effort should be made to exclude malignancy by recent imaging, endoscopy and laboratory markers, including tumour markers such as CA 19-9. Monitoring should be in place for these malignancies. Monitoring should include measuring tumour markers and imaging, e.g. annual MRI.

rules.rule_text: For the indication of cholestatic pruritus in PBC and PSC, separate studies in each disease should be performed.
rules.source_section: 6. Treatment of cholestatic pruritus in PBC and PSC
rules.verbatim_quote: For the indication of cholestatic pruritus in PBC and PSC, separate studies in each disease should be performed.

rules.rule_text: Patients included in pruritus studies should have a minimum level of severity of pruritus at baseline, i.e. at least moderate symptom burden in order to allow the demonstration of a clinically relevant effect of the new drug. Randomisation should be stratified for use of anti-pruritic treatment.
rules.verbatim_quote: Patients included should have a minimum level of severity of pruritus at baseline, i.e. at least moderate symptom burden in order to allow the demonstration of a clinically relevant effect of the new drug. Randomisation should be stratified for use of anti-pruritic treatment.

rules.rule_text: Other relevant secondary endpoints that should be evaluated are quality of life, sleep impairment, etc. The applicant is advised to seek scientific advice in case a new disease-specific symptom and/or health-related quality of life scale is proposed. Bile acids are recommended to be measured as exploratory endpoints when applicable.
rules.verbatim_quote: Other relevant secondary endpoints that should be evaluated are quality of life, sleep impairment, etc. The applicant is advised to seek scientific advice in case a new disease-specific symptom and/or health-related quality of life scale is proposed. Bile acids are recommended to be measured as exploratory endpoints when applicable.
